harness.en: a set of straps used to attach something to a person or animal, or to hold a person safely

ex.en: Climbers must wear a harness for safety.
ex.ko: 등반가들은 안전을 위해 안전벨트를 착용해야 한다.

harness.en: control and use the power of something, especially a natural force

ex.en: The company harnesses wind energy to produce electricity.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 풍력 에너지를 이용해 전기를 생산한다.

hasty.en: done too quickly, without enough care or thought

ex.en: He made a hasty decision and regretted it later.
ex.ko: 그는 성급한 결정을 내렸고 나중에 후회했다.

hatch.en: (of a young bird, fish, or insect) break out of an egg

ex.en: The chicks hatched after three weeks.

hatch.en: an opening in a floor, wall, or roof, or its cover, especially in a ship or aircraft

ex.en: The sailors climbed out through the hatch.

hatch.en: think up and develop a plan, especially a secret one

ex.en: They hatched a plan to escape.

hatred.en: a very strong feeling of dislike

ex.en: His hatred for injustice drove him to act.
ex.ko: 불의에 대한 그의 증오는 그를 행동하게 만들었다.

haunt.en: stay in someone's mind in a troubling way

ex.en: The image of the accident haunted him for months.
ex.ko: 그 사고의 장면이 몇 달 동안 그를 계속 괴롭혔다.

haunt.en: (of a ghost) appear in a place regularly

ex.en: People say the old house is haunted.
ex.ko: 사람들은 그 오래된 집에 유령이 출몰한다고 말한다.

haunt.en: a place that someone visits often

ex.en: This cafe is one of my favorite haunts.
ex.ko: 이 카페는 내가 자주 가는 곳 중 하나다.

haven.en: a place that provides safety or shelter

ex.en: The shelter became a haven for stray animals.
ex.ko: 그 보호소는 유기 동물들의 안식처가 되었다.

hazard.en: something that could be dangerous or cause harm

ex.en: Smoking is a serious health hazard.

hazard.en: say something such as a guess, even though it might be wrong

ex.en: I'll hazard a guess that she's over forty.
ex.ko: 그녀가 마흔 살이 넘었다고 추측해 보겠다.

headline.en: the title of a newspaper article, printed in large letters

ex.en: The story made the front-page headline.

heal.en: become healthy again after an injury or illness

ex.en: The cut on his hand healed quickly.
ex.ko: 그의 손에 난 상처가 빨리 나았다.

heal.en: make someone or something healthy again

ex.en: The medicine helped heal her wound.
ex.ko: 그 약은 그녀의 상처를 낫게 하는 데 도움이 되었다.

healing.en: having the power to cure or make well again

ex.en: Aloe vera has healing properties.

healing.en: the process of becoming healthy again

ex.en: Healing takes time after surgery.
Loud music can damage your hearing.
ex.ko: 시끄러운 음악은 청력을 손상시킬 수 있다.

hearing.en: an official meeting where evidence is presented to help reach a decision

ex.en: The committee held a hearing on the new law.
ex.ko: 위원회는 새 법안에 관한 청문회를 열었다.

heart attack.en: a sudden serious medical condition in which the heart stops working normally

ex.en: He had a heart attack at the age of fifty.

hearted.en: used at the end of adjectives to describe someone's nature or character, as in 'kind-hearted'

ex.en: She is known as a warm-hearted person.
ex.ko: 그녀는 마음이 따뜻한 사람으로 알려져 있다.

ex.en: We had a hearty breakfast before the hike.
ex.ko: 우리는 하이킹 전에 든든한 아침을 먹었다.

ex.en: They gave us a hearty welcome.

heater.en: a device that produces heat to warm a room or water

ex.en: Turn on the heater, it's cold in here.

heaven.cue: 신·천사가 있는 곳, 완전한 행복의 장소
heaven.en: the place where, in some religions, God is believed to live and good people go after death

ex.en: She believes her grandmother is in heaven now.
ex.ko: 그녀는 할머니가 지금 천국에 있다고 믿는다.

heavenly.en: belonging to or relating to heaven

ex.en: They sang a heavenly hymn in the church.

heel.en: the back part of the foot, below the ankle

ex.en: She has a blister on her heel.

heel.en: the raised part on the bottom of a shoe, under the back of the foot

ex.en: She wore shoes with high heels to the party.

height.en: how tall a person or thing is

ex.en: What is your height?

height.en: the highest point or degree of something

ex.en: At the height of his career, he was world famous.

helmet.en: a hard hat that protects your head

ex.en: Always wear a helmet when riding a bicycle.
ex.ko: 자전거를 탈 때는 항상 헬멧을 쓰세요.

helpless.en: unable to protect or help yourself

ex.en: The baby bird looked helpless on the ground.
ex.ko: 그 아기 새는 땅 위에서 무력해 보였다.

herd.en: a large group of animals of the same kind that live or move together

ex.en: A herd of elephants crossed the river.

herd.en: make people or animals move together in a group

ex.en: The guide herded the tourists onto the bus.

hesitate.en: pause before doing something because you are uncertain

ex.en: She hesitated before answering the question.

hidden.en: kept out of sight or hard to notice

ex.en: There was a hidden camera in the room.

highlight.en: the most interesting or exciting part of something

ex.en: The highlight of the trip was visiting the ancient temple.
ex.ko: 그 여행의 하이라이트는 고대 사원을 방문한 것이었다.

ex.en: The report highlights the need for more funding.
ex.ko: 그 보고서는 더 많은 자금 지원의 필요성을 강조한다.

ex.en: She is highly respected by her colleagues.

hip.en: the part of your body between your waist and your leg, at either side

ex.en: She put her hands on her hips.
That's a really hip cafe downtown.
ex.ko: 그건 시내에 있는 정말 힙한 카페야.

ex.en: The company hired ten new employees this month.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 이번 달에 신입 직원 열 명을 고용했다.

hire.en: pay to use something for a short time (British English)

ex.en: We hired a car for the weekend.

historian.en: a person who studies or writes about history

ex.en: The historian wrote a book about ancient Rome.
ex.ko: 그 역사학자는 고대 로마에 관한 책을 썼다.

historic.en: important enough to be remembered as part of history

ex.en: They signed a historic peace agreement.

historical.en: relating to history or things that happened in the past

ex.en: The museum has many historical documents.
ex.ko: 그 박물관은 많은 역사적 문서를 소장하고 있다.

HIV.en: a virus that attacks the body's immune system and can lead to AIDS

ex.en: He was diagnosed with HIV last year.

holy.en: connected with God or religion; sacred

ex.en: They visited a holy site in Jerusalem.

homeless.en: having no home to live in

ex.en: The charity provides food for homeless people.

homeless.en: people who have no home, referred to as a group

ex.en: The city opened a new shelter for the homeless.
ex.ko: 그 도시는 노숙자들을 위한 새 보호소를 열었다.

honest.en: telling the truth and not cheating or stealing

ex.en: She was honest about her mistake.

honestly.en: in a truthful and sincere way

ex.en: Honestly, I don't think this plan will work.
ex.ko: 솔직히 말해서, 나는 이 계획이 통할 거라고 생각하지 않아.

honesty.en: the quality of being truthful and not cheating

ex.en: Honesty is the most important value in our family.
ex.ko: 정직함은 우리 가족에게 가장 중요한 가치다.

honeymoon.en: a holiday taken by a couple just after they get married

ex.en: They went to Hawaii for their honeymoon.

honeymoon.en: a pleasant early period at the start of a relationship or new job

ex.en: The new manager's honeymoon period ended quickly.
ex.ko: 새 관리자의 허니문 기간은 빨리 끝났다.

hop.en: jump a short distance, especially on one foot

ex.en: The rabbit hopped across the garden.

hop.en: get on or off a vehicle quickly (informal)

ex.en: Let's hop on the next train.

hopeful.en: feeling or expressing hope that something good will happen

ex.en: I'm hopeful that things will improve soon.
ex.ko: 나는 곧 상황이 나아질 거라고 기대한다.

hopefully.en: used to say that you hope something will happen

ex.en: Hopefully, the weather will be nice tomorrow.

hopeless.en: having no hope of success or improvement

ex.en: The situation seemed hopeless.
I'm hopeless at cooking.

horizon.en: the line where the sky seems to meet the land or sea

ex.en: The sun rose above the horizon.

horizon.en: the limit of someone's knowledge, experience, or interest

ex.en: Traveling really broadens your horizons.

horn.en: a device in a vehicle that makes a loud warning sound

ex.en: The driver honked the horn to warn the pedestrian.

horn.en: a hard pointed part that grows from the head of some animals

ex.en: The bull lowered its horns and charged.

horn.en: a brass musical instrument played by blowing

ex.en: She plays the horn in the school orchestra.
The accident was a horrible experience.

horrify.en: make someone feel great shock or fear

ex.en: The violent scene horrified the audience.
ex.ko: 그 폭력적인 장면은 관객들을 소름끼치게 했다.

hostel.en: a cheap place to stay, especially for young travelers

ex.en: We stayed at a hostel to save money.

household.en: a group of people, often a family, who live together in one house

ex.en: The average household spends a lot on groceries.
ex.ko: 평균적인 가정은 식료품에 많은 돈을 쓴다.

housework.en: work such as cleaning that is done in a house

ex.en: She does most of the housework on weekends.

huge.en: extremely large in size or amount

ex.en: They live in a huge house.

humanity.en: all the people in the world, considered as a group

ex.en: Climate change is a threat to all of humanity.
ex.ko: 기후 변화는 인류 전체에 대한 위협이다.

humanity.en: kindness and care shown to other people

ex.en: He treated the prisoners with humanity.

humid.en: having a lot of water vapor in the air

ex.en: Summers here are hot and humid.
His speech was full of humor.

humor.en: the ability to understand and enjoy what is funny

ex.en: She has a great sense of humor.
He gave a humorous speech at the wedding.

hunger.en: the feeling of wanting or needing food

ex.en: Millions of people suffer from hunger every day.
ex.ko: 수백만 명의 사람들이 매일 굶주림에 시달린다.

ex.en: She has a hunger for knowledge.

hunt.en: chase and kill wild animals for food or sport

ex.en: Wolves hunt in packs.
I hunted everywhere for my keys.

hunt.en: the activity of chasing and killing wild animals

ex.en: They went on a hunt in the forest.

hurriedly.en: in a hurried or rushed way

ex.en: She hurriedly packed her bags and left.

hut.en: a small, simple building, often made of wood

ex.en: They built a wooden hut by the lake.

ice hockey.en: a sport played on ice in which two teams try to hit a puck into the other team's goal

ex.en: Canada is famous for ice hockey.

ice skating.en: the activity of moving on ice wearing skates

ex.en: We went ice skating at the rink last winter.

identity.en: who a person is, or the qualities that make them who they are

ex.en: Traveling helped her discover her identity.
ex.ko: 여행은 그녀가 자신의 정체성을 발견하는 데 도움이 되었다.

identity.en: who someone is, especially for official purposes

ex.en: The police confirmed the victim's identity.

identity card.en: a card that shows who you are

ex.en: You must show your identity card at the entrance.

idiom.cue: 단어 그대로의 뜻과 다른 관용 표현
idiom.en: a group of words with a meaning that is different from the meanings of the individual words

ex.en: 'Kick the bucket' is an idiom meaning to die.

idol.en: someone who is admired and loved by many people, especially in music or entertainment

ex.en: She became a teen idol after her first album.
ex.ko: 그녀는 첫 앨범 이후 십 대들의 우상이 되었다.

idol.en: an object, such as a statue, that is worshipped as a god

ex.en: The ancient temple was full of golden idols.
ex.ko: 그 고대 사원은 황금 우상으로 가득했다.

ignore.en: pay no attention to someone or something on purpose

ex.en: He ignored her advice and made the same mistake.
ex.ko: 그는 그녀의 조언을 무시하고 같은 실수를 저질렀다.

illegally.en: in a way that is against the law

ex.en: He was arrested for illegally selling drugs.
ex.ko: 그는 마약을 불법적으로 판매한 혐의로 체포되었다.

illness.en: a disease or period of being sick

ex.en: He missed school due to illness.

illuminate.en: light up something or make it brighter

ex.en: Fireworks illuminated the night sky.

illuminate.en: help make something clearer or easier to understand

ex.en: The documentary illuminates the causes of the war.
ex.ko: 그 다큐멘터리는 전쟁의 원인을 명확히 밝혀준다.

imaginary.en: existing only in the mind, not real

ex.en: Children often have imaginary friends.

imitate.en: copy the way someone behaves, speaks, or looks

ex.en: The comedian imitated the president's voice.

immediate.en: happening or done at once, without delay

ex.en: The company took immediate action after the complaint.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 항의를 받은 후 즉각적인 조치를 취했다.

ex.en: Only immediate family attended the funeral.
Call me immediately if anything happens.
The park is immediately behind the school.
ex.ko: 그 공원은 학교 바로 뒤에 있다.

immigrate.en: come to live permanently in a country that is not your own

ex.en: Her family immigrated to Canada in the 1990s.

immigration.en: the process of coming to live permanently in another country

ex.en: Immigration policy has become a major political issue.
ex.ko: 이민 정책은 중요한 정치적 쟁점이 되었다.

impair.en: damage something or make it worse

ex.en: Lack of sleep can impair your concentration.
ex.ko: 수면 부족은 집중력을 손상시킬 수 있다.

impression.en: the opinion or feeling you have about someone or something

ex.en: She made a good impression at the interview.

impression.en: an amusing imitation of the way a person speaks or behaves

ex.en: He does a great impression of the president.

impressive.en: causing admiration because of its size, quality, or skill

ex.en: Her performance was truly impressive.

improper.en: not suitable or acceptable in a particular situation

ex.en: It would be improper to discuss salaries in public.
ex.ko: 공개적으로 급여를 논하는 것은 부적절할 것이다.

improvement.en: a change that makes something better

ex.en: There has been a big improvement in his English.
ex.ko: 그의 영어 실력에 큰 향상이 있었다.

incident.en: something that happens, especially something unusual or unpleasant

ex.en: The police were called to the scene of the incident.

including.en: used to show that something or someone is part of a group being talked about

ex.en: Several people were injured, including two children.
ex.ko: 어린이 두 명을 포함해 여러 사람이 다쳤다.

income.en: the money you earn from work or investments

ex.en: Her monthly income increased after the promotion.
ex.ko: 승진 후 그녀의 월 소득이 늘었다.

ex.en: Sorry, is now an inconvenient time to call?
Your answer to question three is incorrect.

increasingly.en: more and more as time passes

ex.en: It's becoming increasingly difficult to find a job.
ex.ko: 일자리를 찾기가 점점 더 어려워지고 있다.

incredible.en: impossible or very hard to believe

ex.en: It's incredible that she finished the marathon in two hours.
ex.ko: 그녀가 마라톤을 두 시간 만에 완주했다는 게 믿기지 않는다.

ex.en: The view from the mountain was incredible.

incredibly.en: in a way that is hard to believe

ex.en: She recovered incredibly quickly.
ex.ko: 그녀는 믿을 수 없을 정도로 빨리 회복했다.

ex.en: The movie was incredibly boring.

indefinite article.cue: a, an처럼 막연한 대상을 가리키는 관사
indefinite article.en: the word 'a' or 'an' in English, used before a noun that is not specific

ex.en: 'A' is an indefinite article used before consonant sounds.

independent.en: not controlled or influenced by other people or things

ex.en: She became financially independent after graduation.

indirect.en: not going straight or directly; happening through an intermediate step

ex.en: The company suffered indirect losses from the strike.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 파업으로 간접적인 손실을 입었다.

indirectly.en: in a way that is not direct

ex.en: His comments indirectly criticized the government.

individual.en: a single person, as distinct from a group

ex.en: Every individual has the right to be heard.
ex.ko: 모든 개인은 의견을 말할 권리가 있다.

individual.en: relating to one particular person or thing, not to a group

ex.en: The teacher gave individual attention to each student.
It's raining, so let's stay indoors.
The city has a large industrial area.
ex.ko: 그 도시에는 큰 산업 지역이 있다.

industry.en: the businesses that produce a particular kind of goods or services

ex.en: The car industry has changed a lot in recent years.
ex.ko: 자동차 산업은 최근 몇 년 사이 많이 변했다.

inevitable.en: certain to happen and impossible to avoid

ex.en: Change is inevitable in a growing company.

infection.en: an illness caused by bacteria or a virus entering the body

ex.en: She developed an ear infection.

infinitive.en: the basic form of a verb, often used with 'to' (e.g. 'to go')

ex.en: 'To eat' is the infinitive form of the verb 'eat'.
Please inform us of any changes to your address.

inform.en: tell the police about someone's illegal activities

ex.en: He informed on his former partner to the police.
The lecture was very informative.

ingredient.en: one of the foods or substances used to make a particular dish

ex.en: Flour is the main ingredient in bread.

inhabitant.en: a person who lives in a particular place

ex.en: The city has over a million inhabitants.
ex.ko: 그 도시에는 백만 명이 넘는 주민이 있다.

ex.en: She inhaled deeply before diving into the pool.
ex.ko: 그녀는 수영장에 뛰어들기 전 숨을 깊게 들이쉬었다.

ex.en: My initial reaction was surprise.

initial.en: the first letter of a name

ex.en: He signed the document with his initials.
Initially, I was nervous about the new job.

inject.en: put a liquid into someone's body using a needle

ex.en: The nurse injected the vaccine into his arm.

inject.en: add a new quality or feeling to something

ex.en: The new manager injected fresh energy into the team.
ex.ko: 새 매니저는 팀에 새로운 활기를 불어넣었다.

injury.en: physical harm or damage to the body

ex.en: He suffered a serious injury during the match.
ex.ko: 그는 경기 중 심각한 부상을 입었다.

ink.en: a colored liquid used for writing or printing

ex.en: The pen ran out of ink.

ink.en: formally sign a contract or agreement (informal)

ex.en: The two companies inked a deal last week.

innermost.en: deepest inside, especially of feelings or thoughts

ex.en: She shared her innermost thoughts with her best friend.
ex.ko: 그녀는 가장 친한 친구에게 내면 깊숙한 생각을 털어놓았다.

ex.en: The jury found him innocent.

innocent.en: not having much experience of life and therefore not expecting bad things

ex.en: She has an innocent, childlike smile.
ex.ko: 그녀는 순수하고 아이 같은 미소를 가지고 있다.

innovator.en: a person who introduces new ideas or methods

ex.en: Steve Jobs was known as a great innovator.
ex.ko: 스티브 잡스는 위대한 혁신가로 알려져 있다.

inquiry.en: a question you ask to get information

ex.en: We received several inquiries about the job posting.
ex.ko: 우리는 채용 공고에 대해 여러 건의 문의를 받았다.

inquiry.en: an official investigation into an event or problem

ex.en: The government launched an inquiry into the accident.
ex.ko: 정부는 그 사고에 대한 조사를 시작했다.

ex.en: The court declared him legally insane.
ex.ko: 법원은 그가 법적으로 심신상실 상태라고 판단했다.

ex.en: That's an insane amount of homework for one night.
ex.ko: 하룻밤에 하기엔 말도 안 되게 많은 숙제다.

inscribe.en: cut or write words onto a surface, especially as a permanent record

ex.en: His name was inscribed on the trophy.

inscription.en: words written or carved on something, such as a stone or coin

ex.en: The inscription on the statue was hard to read.
ex.ko: 그 동상에 새겨진 글은 읽기 어려웠다.

insight.cue: 복잡한 것의 본질을 꿰뚫어 보는 능력
insight.en: a clear understanding of a complicated situation or problem

ex.en: The book offers valuable insight into human behavior.
ex.ko: 그 책은 인간 행동에 대한 귀중한 통찰을 제공한다.

insist.en: demand something firmly or refuse to change your opinion

ex.en: She insisted on paying for dinner.

inspection.en: a careful official examination of something

ex.en: The factory passed its safety inspection.

inspire.en: give someone the idea or motivation to do something creative or positive

ex.en: Her story inspired many young writers.
ex.ko: 그녀의 이야기는 많은 젊은 작가들에게 영감을 주었다.

install.en: put something in place and set it up for use

ex.en: We need to install new software on all computers.
ex.ko: 모든 컴퓨터에 새 소프트웨어를 설치해야 한다.

install.en: formally place someone in an important position

ex.en: He was installed as the new president of the club.

instance.en: a particular example or case of something

ex.en: This is just one instance of the problem.
ex.ko: 이것은 그 문제의 한 사례일 뿐이다.

instead of.cue: 어떤 것 대신 다른 것을 선택할 때

ex.en: I'll have tea instead of coffee.

institute.en: an organization set up for a particular purpose, such as research or education

ex.en: She works at a research institute.

institute.en: start or establish something, such as a rule or system

ex.en: The school instituted a new dress code.
ex.ko: 그 학교는 새로운 복장 규정을 도입했다.

instruction.en: detailed information telling you how to do or use something

ex.en: Read the instructions before assembling the desk.

instruction.en: the process of teaching someone something

ex.en: The course provides instruction in basic first aid.
ex.ko: 그 과정은 기초 응급처치 교육을 제공한다.

instructor.en: a person who teaches a skill or subject

ex.en: Our swimming instructor is very patient.
ex.ko: 우리 수영 강사는 아주 인내심이 많다.

insurance.en: an arrangement in which you pay money regularly so a company will pay for loss or damage

ex.en: You should get travel insurance before the trip.
ex.ko: 여행 전에 여행자 보험에 가입해야 한다.

intend.en: have a plan or purpose to do something

ex.en: I intend to finish the report by Friday.

intense.en: very strong or extreme in degree

ex.en: She felt intense pressure before the exam.
ex.ko: 그녀는 시험 전에 극심한 압박을 느꼈다.

intensive.en: involving a lot of effort or work in a short time

ex.en: She took an intensive English course this summer.
ex.ko: 그녀는 이번 여름 집중 영어 과정을 들었다.

intention.en: what you plan or mean to do

ex.en: It was not my intention to hurt you.
He intentionally left out important details.

interact.en: communicate with or react to each other

ex.en: Children learn to interact with others at school.
ex.ko: 아이들은 학교에서 다른 사람들과 소통하는 법을 배운다.

interaction.en: communication or action between two or more people or things

ex.en: Social interaction is important for children's development.

intermediate.en: at a level between beginner and advanced

ex.en: She is taking an intermediate Spanish class.
ex.ko: 그녀는 중급 스페인어 수업을 듣고 있다.

intermission.en: a short break between parts of a performance

ex.en: We got some snacks during the intermission.
ex.ko: 우리는 중간 휴식 시간에 간식을 좀 샀다.

internal.en: existing or happening inside a person, place, or organization

ex.en: The company is conducting an internal investigation.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 내부 조사를 진행하고 있다.

internationally.en: in a way that involves many countries

ex.en: Her novels are known internationally.

interrupt.en: stop someone while they are speaking or doing something

ex.en: Please don't interrupt me while I'm on the phone.
ex.ko: 내가 통화 중일 때는 끼어들지 마세요.

interval.cue: 두 시점 또는 지점 사이의 거리
interval.en: a period of time or distance between two events or points

ex.en: The buses arrive at ten-minute intervals.

interviewee.en: a person who is being interviewed

ex.en: The interviewee answered every question calmly.
ex.ko: 그 인터뷰 대상자는 모든 질문에 침착하게 답했다.

introduction.cue: 서로 모르는 사람을 알게 해주는 것
introduction.en: the act of making people known to each other for the first time

ex.en: Let me make the introductions before we start.

introduction.en: the first part of a book, speech, or piece of writing

ex.en: The introduction explains the purpose of the book.

invasion.en: an act of entering a country or place using military force

ex.en: The invasion led to a long war.

invasion.en: an act of entering somewhere you should not, especially violating privacy

ex.en: Reading her diary felt like an invasion of privacy.
ex.ko: 그녀의 일기를 읽는 것은 사생활 침해처럼 느껴졌다.

invest.en: put money into something in the hope of making a profit

ex.en: He invested his savings in stocks.

investigation.en: an official examination to find out the facts about something

ex.en: Police launched an investigation into the fire.
ex.ko: 경찰은 그 화재에 대한 수사를 시작했다.

invitation.en: a request asking someone to come to an event

ex.en: She sent out invitations for her birthday party.

involuntarily.en: without intending to or being able to control it

ex.en: She involuntarily flinched at the loud noise.
ex.ko: 그녀는 큰 소리에 본의 아니게 움찔했다.

involve.en: include something as a necessary part

ex.en: The job involves a lot of traveling.

involve.en: make someone take part in something

ex.en: They involved the whole team in the decision.
ex.ko: 그들은 팀 전체를 그 결정에 참여시켰다.

involved.en: taking part in or connected with something

ex.en: Many people were involved in the project.

involved.en: having a romantic relationship with someone

ex.en: They have been involved for two years.

iron.en: a strong, hard metal used to make steel and tools

ex.en: The gate is made of iron.

iron.en: a device used to make clothes smooth by pressing them

ex.en: Be careful, the iron is very hot.

iron.en: make clothes smooth using a heated iron

ex.en: She ironed her shirt before the interview.

ironing.en: the activity of making clothes smooth using a heated iron

ex.en: I still have a big pile of ironing to do.
